directions.name: The Trusted Local
directions.description: Full-bleed split-screen hero with a plumber photo on the left and a deep navy panel on the right carrying the headline and trust strip. Navy and warm amber throughout. Rounded card corners with subtle shadows. A suburb name badge persists in the sticky nav. Large stat numerals anchor the social proof strip.
directions.rationale: The navy and amber combination reads as reliable and warm without going corporate-blue. The split-screen hero does what jimmysparkselectrical.com.au does well — face of the business, immediate trust — but with more residential warmth. Montserrat at heavy weight gives the tradesman confidence this brand requires.

directions.name: The Neighbourhood Paper
directions.description: The suburb name runs massive across the top like a newspaper masthead — HAPPY VALLEY in optical-size Fraunces serif with the tagline beneath. Warm paper-stock background throughout. Hairline horizontal rules divide sections with no rounded corners. Middle sections use an unequal two-column newspaper grid. Testimonial pull-quotes displayed as editorial-style large type. Copper CTAs. Flat, print-quality precision.
directions.rationale: Adelaide homeowners in the 35–65 bracket trust community-embedded businesses. A newspaper-masthead style signals 'we've been here forever' and 'we're part of this suburb' — exactly the brand promise. Fraunces optical-size serif headings give authority without corporate distance. The editorial density makes the page feel substantial and local, not a thin landing page.

contrast_axes.typography: optical-size serif display vs. readable body

directions.name: The Trade Mark
directions.description: The phone number is the hero — rendered at ~72px on the right side of a strong horizontal rule system, unmissable for emergency callers. Zero border-radius throughout. The credentials section is designed like a licence certificate — Lic. #333997, ABN, Lifetime Labour Warranty in a formal bordered block. Services display in a tight data-table-style grid. Trade red-orange primary — unexpected in this category, sits in the safety-equipment and trade-signage register.
directions.rationale: Emergency intent is the dominant homepage query. Making the phone number the visual centrepiece is a real design decision, not a default. The licence-certificate credential block directly addresses the 'are you actually licensed?' trust question. Sharp edges and geometric precision signal a professional operation, not a handyman.
